title: cnicus ciliatus
intro: Cnicus ciliatus, also known as woolly knapweed, is a perennial plant species native to Europe and Asia, characterized by its hairy leaves and purple flowers. The plant has been found to possess anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating various health issues. The health benefits of Cnicus ciliatus include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and support the immune system. Herbal preparations of Cnicus ciliatus can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures to leverage its medicinal properties and health benefits.
